title: American Sunscreen Is Stuck in 1999
description: The FDA hasn't approved a new sunscreen filter since 1999. Europe, Asia, and Australia have moved on — their shelves carry more stable, broader-spectrum UVA filters that American consumers simply cannot buy. This piece investigates the regulatory classification decision that created the gap, the FDA's own bloodstream absorption data that complicated the picture further, and what it actually means for your skin when avobenzone degrades in sunlight.
